variants.description: Check if the API key (not the APP key) is valid. If invalid, a 403 is returned.

schema.name: valid
schema.description: Return `true` if the authentication response is valid.

schema.name: errors
schema.description: Array of errors returned by the API.

schema.name: errors
schema.description: Array of errors returned by the API.

entries.code:
# Set your Datadog site and credentials
export DD_SITE="datadoghq.com"
export DD_API_KEY="<DD_API_KEY>"

curl -X GET "https://api.datadoghq.com/api/v1/validate" \
-H "DD-API-KEY: ${DD_API_KEY}" \
-H "Accept: application/json"

entries.code:
"""
Validate API key returns "OK" response
"""

from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v1.api.authentication_api import AuthenticationApi

configuration = Configuration()
with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_instance = AuthenticationApi(api_client)
    response = api_instance.validate()

    print(response)

# Validate API key returns "OK" response

require "datadog_api_client"
api_instance = DatadogAPIClient::V1::AuthenticationAPI.new
p api_instance.validate()
